zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern於1370年出生在愛爾蘭萊音斯特時，他的父親Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster13歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh6歲。他和Brigid Nowlan至少有3個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1431年在愛爾蘭萊音斯特享年61歲。

zh-hans.Ferns, County Wexford, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 懷可福德費恩斯
zh-hans.Ballyleigh, St Mullins, Wexford, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 懷可福德巴利利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern于1370年出生在爱尔兰萊音斯特时，他的父亲Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster13岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh6岁。他和Brigid Nowlan至少有3个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1431年在爱尔兰萊音斯特享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Kavanagh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Aileen, Brendan, Cathal, Conor, Eamon, Keelin, Liam, Michael Sean, Niall, Ronan, Sean Patrick.

Irish: from Caomhánach, an adjectival epithet meaning ‘belonging to (Saint) Caomhán’, whose personal name means ‘little beautiful one’. Etymologically, the first part of the name is the same as that in Kevin .

History: The first known bearer of the epithet was Domhnall Caomhánach (Donal Kavanagh, died in 1175), the son of Diarmait Mac Murchadha (Dermot McMurrough), king of Leinster in the 12th century. Despite being killed at an early age, Domhnall Caomhánach gave his name to a large and important family in Irish history.

variants.name: Kinsella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ciaran, Donal, Finbar, Fintan.

Irish (Wexford and Carlow): Anglicized form of Gaelic Cinnsealach, or occasionally Ó Cinnsealaigh ‘descendant of Cinnsealach’, an adjectival personal name meaning ‘proud, masterful’ or ‘chief warrior’. Cinnsealach can also indicate a member of the older population grouping called Uí Chinnsealaigh; compare the origin of Kavanagh .

variants.name: Kevin
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Caoimhín ‘descendant of Caoimhín’ (the personal name Kevin), a name derived from Caémgein ‘comely birth’ although now regarded as a diminutive of Gaelic caomh ‘comely, beloved’. Compare Kavanagh .

variants.name: Cavanaugh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brennan, Aileen, Dermod, Donal, Patrick Sean.
Irish: variant of   Kavanagh  .

variants.name: Cavanah
meanings: with stress on the first syllable, this is a shortened Anglicized form of Mac Caomhánach, see Kavanagh .

alternatively, with stress on -van-, this is a shortened form of McCavana, from Mac an Mhanaigh, ‘son of the monk’ (from manach ‘monk’). This was a Mayo surname, also Anglicized by translation as Monk and Monks .
Irish:

variants.name: Cavender
meanings: English: most probably a nickname from an Anglo-Norman French equivalent of French chavender ‘chub’ (the fish).
English (of Irish origin): Anglicized form of Kavanagh (and its variants, including Cavenagh) or Cavanna . Compare Cavinder .

History: Hugh Cavenagh (died c. 1709) came from Bristol in England to Westmoreland County, VA, in 1658 as an indentured servant for a five-year period to a Mr. Wills. His descendants spell their name mainly Cavender and Cavinder.

variants.name: Kaveney
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Géibheannaigh or Mac Géibheannaigh, ‘descendant or son of Géibheannach’, a personal name based on géibheannach meaning ‘bound, or captive one’. There may have been some confusion between this name and Kavanagh .

variants.name: Cavanagh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brennan, Fergus.
Irish: variant of   Kavanagh  .

variants.name: Kavanaugh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Oonagh.
Irish: variant of   Kavanagh  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Fern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Fearn  .
German: habitational name from a place called Ferna in the Eichsfeld area.

variants.name: Ferns
meanings: English: variant of Fern with plural or excrescent -s.
Irish: variant of Fearn 3, with excrescent -s. Compare Renehan .

variants.name: Fearn
meanings: English: from Middle English fe(a)rn(e) ‘fern, bracken’ (Old English fearn). The surname may be topographic, denoting someone who lived in a place covered with bracken or ferns, or toponymic, denoting someone from a place so named. See Fern .
Scottish: habitational name from Fern, Angus, or Fearn, Ross and Cromarty.
Irish: adopted for Ó Reannacháin (more traditionally Anglicized as Renahan), on account of its resemblance to raithneach ‘fern’. See Renehan .

variants.name: Farnes
meanings: English: variant of   Fern  .
Norwegian: habitational name from a place so named, from far ‘road, track’ + nes ‘headland, promontory’.

nameMeanings.name: Gerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + wald ‘rule’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. There has been some confusion with Gerard . It died out in England at the end of the 13th century. However, it continued to be popular in Ireland, where it had been brought in the 12th century at the time of Strongbow's invasion. It was used in England in the 17th century and revived in the 19th century, along with several other long-extinct names of Norman, Old English, and Celtic origin, and is now more common than Gerard, which survived all along as an English ‘gentry’ name.

variants.name: Jerrold
meanings: Rare variant of   Gerald  , probably a transferred use of the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern was born in 1370, in Leinster, Ireland, his father, Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster, was 13 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh, was 6. He had at least 3 sons and 4 daughters with Brigid Nowlan. He died in 1431, in Leinster, Ireland, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern est né en 1370, à Leinster, Irlande, son père, Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh, avait 6 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles avec Brigid Nowlan. Il est décédé en 1431, à Leinster, Irlande, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern 1370 in Leinster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster 13 und seine Mutter Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh 6. Er hatte mit Brigid Nowlan mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1431 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Leinster, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern è nato il nel 1370 nel Leinster, Irlanda, il suo padre, Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster, aveva 13 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh, aveva 6 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie con Brigid Nowlan. Morì nel 1431, nel Leinster, Irlanda, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fernが1370年にアイルランドレインスターで生まれた時，父Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinsterは13歳，母Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norraghは6歳であった。彼はBrigid Nowlanとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1431年にアイルランドレインスターで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern(이)가 1370년 아일랜드 레인스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster(은)는 13세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh(은)는 6세였다. 그는 Brigid Nowlan(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1431년 아일랜드 레인스터에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern nasceu em 1370, em Leinster, Irlanda, seu pai, Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster, tinha 13 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh, tinha 6 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas com Brigid Nowlan. Ele faleceu em 1431, em Leinster, Irlanda, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern родился в 1370 г. в местности линстир, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh, 6. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери от Brigid Nowlan. Он умер в 1431 г. в местности линстир, Ирландия в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Gerald Kavanagh of Fern nació en 1370, en Leinster, Irlanda, su papá, Art Mór MacMurrough-Kavanagh King of Leinster, tenía 13 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Calf le Veel of Norragh, tenía 6 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas con Brigid Nowlan. Murió en 1431, en Leinster, Irlanda, a la edad de 61 años.
